title: Clean idempotent init pattern in LoRa L2
description:
lora_l2.c:161-195 (lichen_lora_l2_init) implements idempotent initialization correctly:
- Check-under-mutex pattern prevents TOCTOU race
- Comment explains why (prevents EUI-64 regeneration on double-init)
- SECURITY comment notes the race condition being prevented

This pattern is worth defending because:
1. Both main.c and NET_DEVICE_INIT can call init
2. Naive check-then-lock would allow two threads to both pass and generate different EUI-64s
3. The comment makes the invariant explicit

If someone suggests 'simplifying' by removing the mutex or moving the check outside the lock, push back with the documented race condition.
close_reason: Positive review findings - worth defending, not fixing
